entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: An important crop species, Glycine max.

entities.name: GLYCINE SOJA
entities.description: The closest wild relative of Glycine max.

entities.name: GENOME ASSEMBLY
entities.description: The construction of reference-quality genome sequences.

entities.name: ANNOTATION
entities.description: The process of identifying genomic features within a genome assembly.

entities.name: WILLIAMS 82
entities.description: A widely used northern line cultivar of soybean.

entities.name: LEE
entities.description: A widely used southern line cultivar of soybean.

entities.name: SINGLE-NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS
entities.description: Single-base pair variations used to compare different accessions.

entities.name: INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The movement of a gene from one species into the gene pool of another.

entities.name: HAPLOTYPE STRUCTURE
entities.description: The arrangement of alleles on a chromosome.

entities.name: US GERMPLASM COLLECTION
entities.description: A collection of soybean genetic resources from the United States.

entities.name: GLOBAL SOYBEAN DIVERSITY
entities.description: The overall genetic variation found in soybean worldwide.

entities.name: PAN-GENE COLLECTION
entities.description: The entire set of genes found within a species.

entities.name: GENE FAMILIES
entities.description: Groups of genes with similar sequences and functions.

entities.name: INVERSIONS
entities.description: Chromosomal segments that are reversed in orientation.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION LOCI
entities.description: Specific genomic regions associated with the domestication of a species.

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Alternative forms of a gene at a specific locus.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild plants for human use.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing crop traits for better yield or quality.

entities.name: UNITED STATES
entities.description: The country where the Williams 82 and Lee cultivars are used.

relationships.description: Reference-quality assemblies were constructed for soybean accessions
Reference-quality assembly was constructed for Glycine soja
A genome assembly was provided for the Williams 82 line
A genome assembly was provided for the Lee line
Annotations were provided for soybean accessions
Williams 82 is a cultivar of soybean
Lee is a cultivar of soybean
Glycine soja is the closest wild relative of soybean
SNPs were used to compare Williams 82 with other lines
SNPs were used to compare Lee with other lines
SNPs were used to compare Glycine soja with domesticated lines
SNP distributions highlight patterns of introgression in Williams 82 parents
SNP distributions highlight patterns of introgression in Lee parents
SNP distributions highlight haplotype structure in Williams 82 parents
SNP distributions highlight haplotype structure in Lee parents
The collection is used to place accessions relative to global soybean diversity
The study relates sequenced accessions to global soybean diversity
Analysis was performed on a soybean pan-gene collection
Variation in the pan-gene collection occurs primarily in clustered gene families
Inversions were found between Glycine soja and domesticated lines
Inversions were found between Williams 82 and other lines
Inversions were found between Lee and other lines
Domestication loci were compared between Glycine soja and domesticated accessions
Domestication loci were compared between wild and domesticated soybean
Two different alleles were found for each domestication locus
Domestication is a process that shaped soybean
Genome assemblies serve as a basis for soybean crop improvement
Williams 82 is a US cultivar
Lee is a US cultivar
